vulnerability.title: Disable SSH Support for User Known Hosts
vulnerability.description: SSH can allow system users to connect to systems if a cache of the remote systems public keys is available. This should be disabled. To ensure this behavior is disabled, add or correct the following line in /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf: IgnoreUserKnownHosts yes
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core; then mkdir -p /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d touch /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf chmod 0600 /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf LC_ALL=C sed -i "/^\s*IgnoreUserKnownHosts\s\+/Id" "/etc/ssh/sshd_config" LC_ALL=C sed -i "/^\s*IgnoreUserKnownHosts\s\+/Id" "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d"/*.conf if [ -e "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf" ] ; then LC_ALL=C sed -i "/^\s*IgnoreUserKnownHosts\s\+/Id" "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf" else touch "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf" fi # make sure file has newline at the end sed -i -e '$a\' "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf" cp "/etc/s

triage_decision.reason: Disabling SSH support for user known hosts prevents potential bypass of authentication via trusted host keys, reducing risk of unauthorized access.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; networking affected; SSH user known hosts bypass; Unauthorized access via trusted keys

previous_attempts.scan_output: Single-rule scan failed for sshd_disable_user_known_hosts (rule=xccdf_org.ssgproject.content_rule_sshd_disable_user_known_hosts). Could not verify.

reasoning_messages.reasoning:
We need to output remediation commands in the required format. Steps: 1) Identify the finding. 2) Determine the remediation command. 3) Format the answer. The finding: Title: Disable SSH Support for User Known Hosts. So the remediation is to enable SSH support for user known hosts. The command: sed -i "/^\s*IgnoreUserKnownHosts\s\+/Id" "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/*.conf". But we must output exactly as: 

echo '<command> <parameter> <value>'

So we output: echo 'sed -i "/^\s*IgnoreUserKnownHosts\s\+/Id" "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/*.conf"'

But note: The parameter is the entire string including quotes? The example shows: parameter=example_parameter_1 value_1. So we output the parameter name and then the value. In our case, the parameter is the sed pattern and the replacement string? Actually the command is sed -i, the parameter is the pattern/replacement? The example: they wrote <parameter=example_parameter_1>value_1</parameter>. So we should output: echo 'sed -i "/^\s*IgnoreUserKnownHosts\s\+/Id" "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/*.conf"'

Let's produce exactly that.
